content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration in autonomous agents can be adapted to actively chunk and compress unprocessed information within a language model's working memory, directly mitigating the reasoning limits imposed by fixed working memory capacity. This works because ICM rewards agents for exploring uncertain, unconnected reasoning fragments, which incentivizes the agent to group related dispersed information into compact, retrievable chunks that fit within existing working memory bounds.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
